steps.title: Force-Push Mutated Version Tag
steps.text: Attacker force-pushes a mutated version tag (e.g., v0.69.3) to redirect builds to malicious code.
steps.description: The attacker exploits lax tag protection policies to force-push a modified tag pointing to a commit containing backdoored code. Downstream CI/CD pipelines pinned to this tag will now build the compromised version.
steps.expectedOutcome: Builds now reference attacker-controlled code via the mutated tag
steps.detectionHints: Monitor GitHub audit logs for forced tag pushes, verify tag signatures

steps.title: Inject Malicious Build Step
steps.text: Backdoored code injects a malicious step into the CI/CD workflow that runs during build.
steps.description: The compromised codebase includes a modified workflow file or build script that executes attacker code during the CI pipeline run. This step may download additional payloads, modify build outputs, or establish persistence.
steps.expectedOutcome: Arbitrary code execution within the CI runner environment
steps.detectionHints: Monitor workflow file changes, detect unexpected process execution in runners

steps.title: Exfiltrate Secrets from Runner Environment
steps.text: Malicious build step harvests CI/CD secrets, tokens, and SSH keys from the runner.
steps.description: The attacker code reads environment variables containing GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS credentials, Docker registry passwords, SSH keys, and other secrets commonly available in CI runners. Secrets are staged for exfiltration.
steps.expectedOutcome: CI/CD secrets and credentials captured from runner environment
steps.detectionHints: Monitor for SSH key creation in runner directories, unusual env var access

steps.title: Publish Tampered Artifact to Registry
steps.text: Attacker publishes a backdoored build artifact to the container/package registry.
steps.description: The compromised pipeline produces a tampered artifact (container image, npm package, or binary) and publishes it to the legitimate registry using stolen credentials. The artifact appears authentic but contains the backdoor.
steps.expectedOutcome: Tampered artifact published under legitimate version in registry
steps.detectionHints: Verify artifact signatures, compare build hashes with expected values

steps.title: C2 Callback from Compromised Pipeline
steps.text: Exfiltrated secrets are sent to attacker C2 via HTTPS from the CI runner.
steps.description: The malicious build step transmits harvested credentials and secrets to an attacker-controlled endpoint over HTTPS, blending with normal CI/CD outbound traffic.
steps.detectionHints: Network monitoring for unusual outbound connections from CI runners

steps.title: Tag Mutation & Secret Exfil Alert
steps.text: Detection rules correlate forced tag push with secret access and unusual file creation.
steps.description: The detection analytics correlate three signals: forced tag push in GitHub audit logs, SSH key file creation in runner home directory, and outbound HTTPS to non-approved domains. A Critical alert fires.
steps.expectedOutcome: SOC receives correlated supply chain attack alert
steps.detectionHints: Sentinel correlation rule, Sigma for file creation, Splunk for network

steps.title: CLAW: Freeze Pipeline & Rotate Secrets
steps.text: Automated playbook disables compromised workflows, rotates all exposed secrets, and quarantines artifacts.
steps.description: The containment playbook: (1) disables the compromised GitHub Actions workflow, (2) revokes all GitHub tokens and PATs, (3) rotates AWS/Docker credentials, (4) quarantines the tampered artifact in the registry, (5) creates P1 incident.
steps.expectedOutcome: Pipeline frozen, secrets rotated, tampered artifacts quarantined

steps.title: Build Provenance Audit
steps.text: SOC analyst verifies build provenance chain and identifies all affected downstream consumers.
steps.description: Full audit of the build provenance: compare artifact hashes against signed manifests, trace all downstream consumers of the tampered artifact, enumerate all environments that pulled the compromised version.
steps.expectedOutcome: Complete blast radius assessment with consumer list
steps.detectionHints: Registry pull logs, deployment manifests, SBOM analysis

steps.title: Artifact Recall Decision
steps.text: CISO decides: full artifact recall vs. targeted remediation.
steps.description: With confirmed supply chain compromise, the CISO must decide whether to initiate a full artifact recall affecting all consumers or targeted remediation of known-affected systems only.
steps.expectedOutcome: Decision documented with rationale and timeline

cisoOptions.label: Full Artifact Recall
cisoOptions.impact: All consumers notified, artifact pulled from registry — 8h disruption

cisoOptions.label: Targeted Remediation
cisoOptions.impact: Only confirmed-affected systems patched — 2h, residual risk

steps.title: Enforce Tag Protection & SLSA Build Provenance
steps.text: Deploy tag protection rules, artifact signing, and SLSA Level 3 build provenance verification.
steps.description: Apply governance controls: enable tag protection on all release branches, require signed commits and tags, enforce SLSA Level 3 build provenance for all artifacts, implement Sigstore cosign verification in deployment pipelines.
steps.expectedOutcome: Hardened CI/CD pipeline with defense-in-depth against supply chain attacks
